notes: Mechanism claim, well-grounded. Named entities, clear conditions, falsifiable premise about capital effects on opportunity access.
Mechanism claim about behavioral accumulation. Clear metaphor (pennies on scale), resolvable conditions, testable threshold concept.
Assertion about order-of-operations sequencing. Named speaker, specific geographic context (Texas), mechanism grounded in infrastructure reuse.
Quantified claim with axis-anchored numbers (market CAGR). Specific industries named, mechanism clear (tailwind enables default growth), falsifiable against market data.

Assertion about constraint diagnosis. Note: conditions array is oversized (37 items, A5-smell), but claim text itself is standalone and load-bearing. Mechanism is clear: marketing accelerates fulfillment failures when supply is constrained.
Assertion grounded in personal example (Alex's early content, 10-13 years prior). Load-bearing claim about compounding from starting imperfectly. Time-anchored, falsifiable via output comparison.
Assertion about margin vs. volume tradeoff. Clear mechanism (margin-per-unit dominates), grounded in pricing context, falsifiable through revenue modeling.
Mechanism claim about funnel design. Medical consulting context named, specific problem (all leads → scheduler), mechanism is call-slot crowding, testable.
Assertion about short-form/long-form viewer behavior. Named speaker (Hormozi), Acquisition.com context, conversion events specified, testable via cohort tracking.
Conditional claim with time axis (9 PM bedtime, 5 AM wake, 8-hour window). Mechanism is upstream-vs-downstream control. Falsifiable through sleep consistency measurement.
Mechanism claim about brand-as-association. Concrete example (Nike vs. white t-shirt, ~12x premium), mechanism grounded in symbolic association, falsifiable through pricing data.
Assertion about sunk-cost fallacy in founder decisions. Named speaker, mechanism is rational capital allocation, falsifiable through decision analysis.

Assertion about personal heuristic (epic story razor). Named speaker (Hormozi), specific decisions cited (Gym Launch, Layla, gym residence, media company), bounded as self-reported heuristic. Falsifiable via pattern-matching against observed choices, though subjectivity of 'epic' is noted in bounded_by.
